item.title: To save the midterms, Trump needs victory in Iran
item.description: Americans will endure high gas prices for a good military outcome — but not for a bad deal.

item.title: Don’t believe Trump and Xi’s narrative. America keeps falling uphill.
item.description: Hold off on those announcements of the dawn of the Chinese century.

item.title: At last, civics is making a college comeback. More, please.
item.description: Most colleges don't require a civics class to graduate, but students sorely need it.

item.title: What Thomas Massie's loss means for MAGA
item.description: After Thomas Massie's primary defeat, what does the future hold for the GOP and the MAGA movement? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Kate Andrews, James Hohmann and Robert Gebelhoff discuss the political cost of breaking from President Donald Trump and whether Republicans are speeding toward a midterms disaster.

item.title: The unintended consequences of mass deportations
item.description: The administration say immigration crackdowns are good for native-born workers. A new study shows otherwise.

item.title: The climate apocalypse? Don’t count on it.
item.description: Researchers finally retire the most extreme scenarios that have informed policy and media coverage for years.

item.title: The Supreme Court refuses to ‘toy with’ protecting democracy
item.description: The justices retreat on voting rights in favor of a do-nothing doctrine.

item.title: How to protect the economy from the ghosts of 1979
item.description: Kevin Warsh is right to examine Fed models and approach on fiscal policy.

item.title: Men are falling behind. Let's talk about it.
item.description:
Boys and men are struggling across some of the most important measures: education, employment, family life and mental health. But too often, attempts to talk seriously about these problems get pulled into a culture-war fight. So, what’s actually happening, who is struggling and how we can help them without the conversation being framed as men versus women?
Host Megan McArdle is joined by Richard Reeves, author of "Of Boys and Men," to break down the reality of the gender divide.

item.title: Why the Pentagon is transforming the nation’s suite of weapons
item.description: The U.S. can prepare for the next strait crisis with cheaper drones.

item.title: Why Bernie Sanders is wrong about Sweden
item.description: Why do progressives like Sen. Bernie Sanders love Scandinavia so much? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Damir Marusic, Adam O'Neal and Dominic Pino debunk the myth of the Swedish socialist utopia.
